source.submodule: Array of structure and nested structure

topicTags: Array of structure and nested structure

content.problemStatement:
An array of structures is a data structure in C programming that allows you to create a collection of multiple structures of the same type. \
Each element of the array is a structure with its own set of members. \
This concept combines the features of both arrays and structures, enabling you to organize and manage a list of related data entities.

In an array of structures, you create multiple instances of a structure type and store them in a sequential manner within an array. \
This is particularly useful when you need to handle multiple instances of similar data, such as a list of students, employees, books, or any other entities with common attributes.

Check the code given in the IDE
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 10^3$
- $1 \leq N \leq 10^3$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ over all testcases does not exceed $2\cdot 10^3$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of books present in the library.
- Information about each book consists of 3 lines of input.
    - The first line contains the name of the book
    - The second line contains the Purchase order number - PO number
    - The third line contains the quantity of the book purchased
content.outputFormat:
Output the following for the books present in the library
- Book name
- Count of books
